event_description: Two similar white motor yachts travel near each other. EO shows separate hulls and wakes that run roughly parallel and later interact or cross. IR shows two warm hulls and distinct wake trails, making identity preservation and non-merging important.

qa.question: In the early EO segment, which cue pairing is visible for the object?

options.A: the object with two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; the crossing wake pattern nearby
options.B: the object with the crossing wake pattern; two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths nearby
options.C: the object with shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; bright water or fixed shoreline texture nearby
options.D: the object with bright water or fixed shoreline texture; wake shape next to the hull nearby

qa.evidence_note: separate hulls and roughly parallel wakes later interact or cross

qa.question: Across the EO scale or angle change, which motion cue stays paired with the object?

options.A: the crossing wake pattern; view scale or angle changes
options.B: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; view scale or angle changes
options.C: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; view scale or angle changes
options.D: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; view scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: separate hulls and roughly parallel wakes later interact or cross

qa.question: In IR, which contrast pairing is visible near the object?

options.A: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; shoreline, glare, or open-water texture
options.B: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; the crossing wake pattern
options.C: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths
options.D: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; bright water or fixed shoreline texture

qa.evidence_note: two warm hulls and distinct wake trails support non-merging

qa.question: Which nearby cue creates a same-scene confusion?

options.A: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; near the object
options.B: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; near the object
options.C: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; near the object
options.D: the crossing wake pattern; near the object

qa.evidence_note: collapsing two similar yachts into one identity when wakes cross

qa.question: Which EO/IR cue pair is visible?

options.A: EO: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; IR: bright water or fixed shoreline texture
options.B: EO: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.C: EO: the crossing wake pattern; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.D: EO: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: separate hulls and roughly parallel wakes later interact or cross; IR: two warm hulls and distinct wake trails support non-merging

qa.question: Which cue pairing continues through camera/view change?

options.A: the object: the crossing wake pattern; screen scale or angle changes
options.B: the object: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; screen scale or angle changes
options.C: the object: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; screen scale or angle changes
options.D: the object: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; screen scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: separate hulls and roughly parallel wakes later interact or cross

qa.question: Which EO cue is aligned with the IR contrast cue?

options.A: EO cue: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; IR cue: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.B: EO cue: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; IR cue: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.C: EO cue: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; IR cue: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.D: EO cue: the crossing wake pattern; IR cue: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: separate hulls and roughly parallel wakes later interact or cross; IR: two warm hulls and distinct wake trails support non-merging

qa.question: Which competing cue appears in the cross-modal chain?

options.A: EO/visible cue: the crossing wake pattern; IR/contrast context: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.B: EO/visible cue: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; IR/contrast context: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.C: EO/visible cue: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; IR/contrast context: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.D: EO/visible cue: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; IR/contrast context: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast

qa.evidence_note: collapsing two similar yachts into one identity when wakes cross

qa.question: When the view changes, which cue is checked against the nearby alternative?

options.A: the crossing wake pattern; two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths
options.B: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths
options.C: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; the crossing wake pattern
options.D: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; the crossing wake pattern

qa.evidence_note: separate hulls and roughly parallel wakes later interact or cross

qa.question: Which IR cue pairing appears in this interval?

options.A: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; the crossing wake pattern
options.B: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; shoreline, glare, or open-water texture
options.C: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths
options.D: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast; bright water or fixed shoreline texture

qa.evidence_note: two warm hulls and distinct wake trails support non-merging

qa.question: Which combined EO/IR sequence account is visible?

options.A: EO: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.B: EO: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.C: EO: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast
options.D: EO: the crossing wake pattern; IR: warm hull or disturbed-water contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: separate hulls and roughly parallel wakes later interact or cross; IR: two warm hulls and distinct wake trails support non-merging

qa.question: For the full clip, which cue chain is visible?

options.A: the object: the crossing wake pattern; nearby cue: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths
options.B: the object: shoreline, glare, or open-water texture; nearby cue: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths
options.C: the object: two-hull continuity through interacting wake paths; nearby cue: the crossing wake pattern
options.D: the object: bright water or fixed shoreline texture; nearby cue: the crossing wake pattern

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
